desc:
Your legs tire of constantly ascending and descending these mountain slopes. The
grey rock that forms the road is loose and jagged, and every step must be
watched lest you injure yourself. Aside from several small scrubs and hardy
grasses, you can find no life here. The road has fallen into heavy disrepair
here, but heavy foot prints can still be seen. A smaller path descends
northwards.
name: A Crumbling Road

desc:
A trail runs through here, covered with greenish moss and slightly slippery. It
seems the path has not been used in some time. Both to the north and to the
south it winds into the mountains, steeply to the south, more gently northwards.
name: Mossy Path

desc:
The grey slate once more resembles a road and seems to lead somewhere. A dark
ominous tower, almost blending in with the mountains as backdrop can be glimpsed
to the east. The road also heads west here.
name: A Broken Road

desc:
The grey rock of the road comes to a halt before a massive tower made of the
same slate grey rock of the road. Mountains abound on all sides, and any
entrance further east becomes decidedly tougher. The tower itself seems to be in
disrepair, but all signs also indicate recent occupants. The road continues to
the west and the din of a river relentlessly punishing stone occasionally drifts
from the south. More mountains stretch east, inhospitable as ever. A small metal
plaque has been fixed to the tower wall, beside the door.
name: A Road through the Grey Mountains

desc:
The trail continues, dodging some large, dense bushes and particularly
impressive pine. Here it is cool and quiet, with only a slight breeze disturbing
the stillness. High above, patches in the canopy allow you to see some sky.
name: Mossy Path

desc:
The air about you is soothing; and far away indeed seem the cares and dangers of
the Ettenmoors. The cold light of the sun is shaded by several old pine- trees
that border the path, and these same pines shelter you from the chill wind; but
the tops of the trees sway back and forth, paying that much homage to the icy
blasts. The ground grows rockier to the north, and the moss that covers the
ground begins to give way to tougher grasses.
name: Mossy Path

desc:
Strange tracks on the ground lead to the north. You have descended a bit in
elevation from when you were east of here, and you are now surrounded completely
by mountains. From just to your west, you can hear screams. Or maybe it's just
wolves howling. How can you be sure though?
name: Grey Mountains

desc:
Phew! You're finally there. Walking in the mountains, you can see all the lands
around you. Far below you see the river, winding along from the eerie Misty
Mountains in the far east to the safer parts of the world in the west. Next to
the river you can see a small trail.
name: Grey Mountains

desc:
Up and up you go. Soon you will be among the mountains themselves, but here you
are only about halfway up. You are climbing up in a small ravine that was carved
by a stream running down from the mountains, so you aren't able to see any of
the surrounding lands yet. Just a little higher and you'll be there.
name: Climbing into the Grey Mountains

desc:
The foothills are getting bigger and steeper. Soon you will be climbing up the
eastern face of the Grey Mountains. To continue on, you must climb up the steep,
darkly coloured stone. It looks quite dangerous from here, but the view might be
worth it.
name: Climbing into the Grey Mountains

desc:
Clambering up the boulders from the river below, you find yourself among some
foothills made of dark stone that rise up sharply to the west. To your east, you
can make out the river far below. The rock face to your north makes passage
impossible. You can faintly hear strange noises coming from the mountains to
your west.
name: Foothills of the Grey Mountains

desc:
Here, the river is very wide and shallow, making a crossing without a boat
possible. The water is clear and you can see what might be bones among the
stones at the bottom of the river. To the south a path continues on, heading for
the mountains to the east. To the north, boulders have fallen from the mountains
all the way down to the banks of the river, and you can probably make your way
among them if you are careful.
name: A Shallow Section of the River

desc:
The trail is now at the same elevation as the river. To the north, the river
looks peaceful and easy to ford. You can continue to follow the trail east or
west. You can also enter some foothills to the south if you are feeling VERY
brave.
name: Trail Meets a Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
Maybe you should have headed into the foothills. You are almost overcome with
dread and fear as you survey the barren lands around you. Shrill screams sink
down to your alert ears from the mountains and hills around you. The calm and
safety that you knew so well just to the west of here are just a memory now long
gone...
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
The trail splits in two and heads south and north. To the north, it continues
along high above the river. To the south, it slowly disappears as it climbs up
into the Misty Mountains. To the east, a cliff wall keeps you from being able to
ascend to the mountain tops above you.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
It's never too late to turn back. It's beginning to look like it might be too
late very soon. You know nothing has changed in the last few minutes, but every
time you look up, the sky seems darker. The worn trail runs carelessly to the
east and west. Your passage is hopelessly blocked to the north and south by
dense growth.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Far below you, you can see rough whitewater rapids in the river. It looks like a
very dangerous place. The trail here is well-used. Along it, you see dark spots
that look suspiciously like blood. A piece of skin and a clump of fur (or is it
hair?) is lying on the ground.
name: A Trail above the Rapids

desc:
Your whole body suddenly feels cold and icy. The temperature has not fallen, but
something dark and cold has overcome you. You can't help but feel wild and
hopeless despair as you look to the east. It is hard to see very far in any
direction. You aren't sure if it is foggy or if there is something wrong with
you.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
South of here you can see the Worn Foothills. They seem to curse this land with
their barren loneliness. A cold wind blows from the east, making you shiver. The
trail is worn into the ground here, and the steep hills to your south block your
passage. To your north, you can see a river flowing steadily along. The river's
bank is too steep to climb down.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
The trail has been partly washed away. It's still passable, but don't get too
close to the edge. The trail goes east and south and the northwest edge has been
completely washed down into the river far below. You can only pray that it
doesn't rain again before you come back this way, because the trail might not be
here much longer.
name: A Washed Out Trail

desc:
If you continue east, you will be wandering in the foothills of the Misty
Mountains. The trail you are on continues to the north. You hear strange sounds
and cries rising out of the foothills. Panic begins to cloud your thinking.
Maybe it would be best if you hurried on past this place.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
Cutting through dense vegetation, the trail winds and twists and it rises up to
the mountains in the east. Looking just to the east, you can see that you will
soon be walking among some small foothills. What you can see of the trail takes
a crazy path up through the foothills.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Your legs burn from the steep rise. How much farther, you wonder, could this
accursed trail rise? How many boulders could there be... never had you imagined
so many of the big black things had existed. Each one blocks the trail, or is
placed such as to confound attempts at travel. And all around, the grey dead
lands brood vacantly.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Now you travel up a rocky incline, and not as the crow flies. For each step to
the east, another three is taken to the north, and a quick knee-scraping
scramble up. Movement here is maddeningly slow, but not life-threatening. You
couldn't fall more than a few feet in these harsh rocks. You almost wish for
some danger, for some relief from the stifling, monotonous climb.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Here the trail straightens out a bit, and you can see what lies ahead, finally.
No forest, no city -- only a trail sprawls out to either side, for what seems
miles. The very wind here seems dull and dead, the rocks seem less alive than
granite could ever be. The only thing more repulsive than this roadway is the
insanely dark and broken lands surrounding it.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
For miles to the east and west this river is an unbroken straight blade, shining
through the greyness of the mountains and badlands. But here, a small
outcropping to the north forces the river to skirt the rock. Roiling and
spraying, the water beats futiley against this stone protrusion, but to no
avail. Dejectedly it still flows westward.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
Although the grey mountainsides to the north form a perfectly smooth and strong
barrier for the water, slowly and patiently the rock is being eaten away, and
carried far to the west and south. Alas, this labour is not without toll, and
the water you are on grows bitter and unclean with the dust and lime.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
Gurgling and bubbling, the river flows guardedly west, backing away from the
tortured and cruel mountains of the east. In the murmurs of the river, you
imagine that the water is trying desparately to relate its tales of the east,
tales filled with horror and evil to cause even the most stalwart hero to cringe
like a craven slave.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
The water here is somewhat clearer than it was further to the east. The river is
a bit wider but it is also more shallow. Logs stuck in the river and large rocks
poking up make passage difficult. The banks of the river are covered with dense
foliage that makes it impossible to see the surrounding lands, in in addition to
preventing you from leaving the river.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
The river turns again to the east here, flowing along to the south of the Grey
Mountains. Its colour has become quite dark due to all the debris being washed
into it from small streams in the mountains. Through the murky water you can
catch brief glimpses of dark moving shapes. Or is that just your imagination?
name: Bend in the River

desc:
The rapids that are further upstream have disappeared entirely. The river is
calm and the water rushes along silently. You are in the shadow of the Grey
Mountains that rise up high above you just to your north. The water is dark and
dirty, and a feeling of hopelessness slowly settles on you.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
Ahhh, how refreshing. The soft sound of these peaceful rapids soothes your
aching soul. Even though you are still worried by the evil you feel about you
and the strange shapes you sometimes see moving along the banks, you are able to
take a moment to appreciate this calm and peaceful place.
name: Peaceful Rapids

desc:
Although there are a few rocks and other dangerous objects in the river, you
momentarily lose yourself in the gentle undulating of these gentle rapids. Your
worries and fears disappear for the time being in this enjoyable experience.
name: Whitewater Rapids

desc:
Perhaps continuing to this point in the river was not such a great idea. You are
barely able to keep control of your boat as you are bounced between hazardous
rocks and boulders that have fallen from the heights. The river looks calmer
both to the east and west. In fact, anything else looks better than this.
name: Rough Whitewater Rapids

desc:
The water gushes along, inexorably carving a path into a dark stone, forming a
deep cleavage into the already tall mountains. To the west, you can hear the
sounds of some rapids. On towards the east, the river widens and becomes more
shallow. Fortunately to the west, the rapids do not seem too dangerous.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
Around and around swirls the water in the river, caught in numerous eddies
created as the river twists and turns through the foothills of the mountains to
the northwest. The river cuts through the dark stone that rises up to form the
Grey Mountains high above you. You keep telling yourself you are not afraid of
the wild mountains and the ominous river, but it does no good.
name: Bend in the River

desc:
Shaping the course of the river, the Grey Mountains rise up to the west. The
river bends and winds around them. Steep rock rises up on both sides of the
river. The looming Grey Mountains make your whole body feel icy.
name: Bend in the River

desc:
The water here rushes west at a furious pace, as if trying to escape something
horrible in the east. Its clarity gives no hint of the terrors it must have seen
as it washed down out of the Misty Mountains. The banks of the river are steep
and slick, giving no hope of climbing them. The river flows westward to the Grey
Mountains.
name: On a River

desc:
The water here rushes west at a furious pace, as if trying to escape something
horrible in the east. Its clarity gives no hint of the terrors it must have seen
as it washed down out of the Misty Mountains. The banks of the river are steep
and slick, giving no hope of climbing them. Although this is a peaceful place,
you feel a sense of dread and despair begin to shadow your soul.
name: On a River

desc:
The mountains rule unchallenged here. In fact, as you peer south and east and
north, you can see nothing but these grey mountains. Only from a peak could you
possibly see the land bowing below. The wind is as strong as ever here, whipping
your hair about and stinging every exposed piece of skin. You find it leaves a
bitter taste in your mouth.
name: Grey Mountains

desc:
Your eyes, longing for the majestic beauty of the cliffs you gazed on before,
find no solace in this horrid grey landscape. And the ground before the cliffs,
which seemed like torture to your legs, was unspeakably kinder than what you
tread upon now.
name: Grey Mountains

desc:
Sheer, impassable walls of grey stone rise up to the east, preventing any travel
beyond them. Impassively they look down upon you and judge whether your
existence is worthy...and deign not to inform you of the decision. An icy blast
of wind is the only answer you hear to your pleas for mercy from this dangerous
clime.
name: Grey Mountains

desc:
You find yourself in a large cavern sunk deep into the face of the mountains.
The air is cool and clammy and there is a stench that you can't quite identify.
There are signs that someone or something has been here recently. Maybe you
should leave before it comes back.
name: Cavern in the Grey Mountains

desc:
Farther into the rocky badlands this trodden trail ventures. Huge foot-prints,
not even slightly concealed, travel both to the south and north. Several of the
tracks have freshly broken the soil. Far to the south and east the Misty
Mountains form from smaller, fragmented hills.
name: Dirty Trail

desc:
Here, on a turn in the trail, the foot and paw prints increase, until the ground
seems just layer after layer of prints. A hole, in the middle of the trail, has
been dug out of the rocky ground. The stench about here reeks of old beasts and
filth. Scattered about the hole are various corpses and skeletons, in various
stages of decay. Suddenly, a startling growl is heard from under the soil.
name: Dirty Trail

desc:
The trail lumbers east and north, trampling though the barren wastes that
surround you. Jagged rocks and broken stones threaten your feet, yet you notice
that whatever made the tracks on this trail seem to have been bothered little by
the edges and splinters on the ground.
name: Dirty Trail

desc:
South the trail continues, into a debatable land in which no life exists. North
this trampled swath joins with the main trail, however that seems hardly more
enticing to either your legs or your intuition. A rotten corpse, too frozen and
dry to attract flies, stains the area with a horrible stench.
name: Dirty Trail

desc:
You can go no further in this direction. As much as you don't want to, you must
go back the way you came, through the Worn Foothills. To the north and west, the
foothills become too rough to be passable. To the south, the Misty Mountains
rise up so steeply that you don't even consider climbing them.
name: West of the Worn Foothills

desc:
Wandering through these foothills is beginning to wear on your soul. You long
for the simple warmth and safety that you once knew. West of here, the hills get
a bit smaller. They look more passable than the ones you are currently in.
Heading east will bring you deeper into the Worn Foothills.
name: Worn Foothills

desc:
Ick. These hills are a sorry sight. The hills are made of dense, wet, and black
dirt. The whole place smells putrid, as if many creatures have died and decayed
here. The hills continue to the east and rise up to some mountains south of
here. In many places, blood seems to have been mixed in with the dirt on the
ground.
name: Worn Foothills

desc:
Why would anyone want to live out here? Well, it seems that someone did because
just east of here you can see a small hut. From here, it looks abandoned. Looks
can be deceiving. Better be careful. If you have to flee through these hills, it
will be rough going.
name: Worn Foothills

desc:
This hut is very old and had obviously been abandoned for a long time. In one
corner is a pile of white dust that could have been a pile of bones many years
ago. The hut itself is beginning to fall apart. It is made of a hard wood that
you can't quite identify. Though the wood is strong, the walls are rotting.
There are odd scrapes on the floor, but don't worry. They too look older than
time...or so you think.
name: An Abandoned Hut

desc:
What a terrible place to be lost in! You wander back and forth, your sense of
direction lost. All you know is that the Misty Mountains seem to surround you no
matter which way you look. Maybe if you head back west, if you can find it, you
will find a way out..
name: Deep in the Worn Foothills

desc:
It smells like something has been dead a long, long time here. The wooden beams
that support the floor of the hut above you are rotting, and you don't feel very
safe here. From the rotted remains of various items here, you postulate that
this was once a wine cellar.
name: Smelly Cellar

desc:
This room is nothing like the cellar you just came from. The walls are made of
an odd polished stone and they look newly made. In some places, runes that you
are unable to translate have been carefully carved. The room seems completely
empty, which is odd because you certainly don't feel alone.
name: Strange Room

desc:
Now high up in the mountains, you would surely have a wonderful view if it
weren't for the mist all around you. You can hear strange calls from below that
make your blood run cold. A faint odour reminds you of trolls. Or is it just the
smell that seems to live in these forsaken mountains?
name: High in the Misty Mountains

desc:
Back and forth, up and down you go as you make your way through the mountains.
You have almost lost all sense of direction. There is a steep but climbable
incline rising up to the west. The mountains are too steep to climb to the north
and south.
name: Wandering in the Misty Mountains

desc:
These mountains rise high above the hills below. You can follow the mountains as
they rise up even further (is that possible?) to the west, or you can attempt to
descend the east face of the mountain. Somehow you don't feel too luck as you
look at the terrain around you.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
There must be nothing that these plains have not seen. From the looks of them,
they have been swept with disease, fire, and even floods at one time. They have
heard many screams and have watched many die in agony. Now you feel that they
are watching you, and that you time is near.
name: Burned Plains

desc:
These terrible plains seem to continue forever to the west. North of you, high
mountains rise up. Going to your east or your south won't get you off these
plains, but at least you can see something in the distance in those directions.
Nothing grows on this plains and, in places, deep scars have been cut into the
ground.
name: Burned Plains

desc:
No longer lost in the Worn Foothills, you are climbing up into the Misty
Mountains. 'Is this really a good idea?' you wonder as you push on. It looks
like being lost in the hills might be a whole lot better than being lost in
these mountains.
name: Entering the Misty Mountains

desc:
Higher still rise the mountains! You are almost climbing straight up now. The
rocky slopes are made of dark but smooth stone. The stone seems to simply absorb
light and it is cold to touch. As you walk along, you pass the remains of some
poor animal that made the mistake of exploring this place.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Phew. Finally among the high tops of the Misty Mountains. To the east, you see
the Misty Mountains rising up from the scared hills and plains below them. The
place you find yourself in is oddly quiet, and the dark stone mountains give you
no comfort.
name: High in the Misty Mountains

desc:
Far below is a dangerous looking cliff. You can scale the mountain side down to
it if you are careful. From this point, you are able to see the peaceful lands
of Arda far to the west. You know that, somewhere out there, is the noble town
of Rivendell, but right now it is hard for you to picture it.
name: High in the Dark Mountains

desc:
You have come down from the mountains as far as you can. If you continue on
down, the mountain side turns into a cliff. Attempting to climb down the cliff
wall will be the last mistake you ever make. Better turn back and pass through
the desolate mountains again. At least you know they are safe...more or less...
name: Dangerous Cliff

desc:
Here you are, all alone and wandering in these dangerous hills. There seems to
be no logic to why you have come here. Even with a large group of adventurers,
this place is by no means safe. Hopelessness clouds your thoughts as you begin
to believe that sooner or later, something here will kill you. Gathering your
courage, you think of a path to take.
name: Burned Hills

desc:
At last you are nearing the end of these ugly hills. Just east of you are some
steep hills. They don't look inviting, but they are certainly better than where
you are now. To your south, you can see a ravine coming down from the mountains,
but it is too steep to climb. South, past the ravine, and to the north, you see
the ever-present Misty Mountains.
name: Burned Hills

desc:
The mountains sink briefly down into steep hills. North and south of you are the
Misty Mountains. To your west, you can enter the Burned Hills. Neither option
looks good to you as you try to decide if you should hike into the mountains or
risk it in the hills.
name: Steep Hills

desc:
Back within the mountains, this is clearly one of the many ranges of the Misty
Mountains. The range continues to the west. This point is so high up here that
attempting to climb down to the hills south of here would require great caution.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
You walk carefully among the mountains. The view of the surrounding lands is
complete in every direction, but you don't dare take you eyes off your path for
fear of losing your footing and falling to your death. The wind seems to whisper
something to you as it whips around the mountains.
name: Climbing in the Misty Mountains

desc:
These mountains certainly do deserve their name! A thick mist hangs in the air
and keeps you from seeing more than a few yards in any direction. You can hear
faint noises from all around you. You imagine you can see shapes moving in the
fog.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Thick but strange grass grows on these plains. The grass is tall and things seem
to be moving about in it. In the distance, you think you can see bears wandering
across the plain, but they could be trolls. To the west, the plains seem to
continue on forever. You exit to the south is blocked by steep mountains.
name: Wild Plains

desc:
Standing on this part of the barren plains, you follow along the mountains that
line the southern horizon. A stiff, chilling wind wails through the mountain
peaks and drives the heat from your bones. Though tired from trudging through
the thick, brownish grass that covers the plains, intuition tells you that this
is not a safe place to rest.
name: Wild Plains

desc:
You are now between two ranges of mountains. You can't see very far up the
mountains because they are shrouded in a thick mist. East of you, you see a gap
between the mountains where you might be safe from the weather and the wild
animals that roam these plains.
name: Wild Plains

desc:
Heading south from the mountains, you have ended up in a small space between two
ranges. You can climb up into the mountain range south of here. In this small
gap, you are safe from the wind and cold that were eating away your heart and
body while you were hiking in the naked mountains.
name: A Gap between the Mountains

desc:
You start your climb up into the foothills, leaving the safety of the trail
behind and below you. To the east, foothills rise into the Misty Mountains.
South and southeast of you, you can see the wild plains between two ranges of
mountains
name: Entering the Foothills

desc:
Up and up go the foothills, leading to the Misty Mountains. To the east you can
climb up into the mountains. The way looks rough and you aren't sure you'll be
able to get down once you get up. The foothills to the south are too rough to be
climbable.
name: Foothills

desc:
You have stepped off the trail and you suddenly discover that you are among the
famous Misty Mountains. What a forsaken place this is! The evil here is thick
and you begin to feel chilly even though you are not very high in the mountains
yet.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
You are now high up in the mountains. An icy wind blows from the east, whipping
across the mountains tops. The lands of Eriador lie to the west far away. You
can see nothing east because the mountains rise up steeply in that direction.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Steep mountain sides block your exit in all directions but south. You can only
hope that none of the monsters you know to exist in these mountains come at this
moment, because you could easily be cornered here. Looking at the high cliffs
that surround you, you find it hard to control your panic. You'd better get down
from here before you drive yourself crazy.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Now high up in the mountains, you are walking along the bank of a river. The
bank to the north is too steep to climb down. The trail is well worn and covered
with animal tracks. There are also some tracks that you can't quite identify.
How strange, you thought you could track anything...
name: A Trail along a River

desc:
Still next to the river, the trail has become a bit wider. It is made of dirt
and rock, and anything that used to grow on it has long since been killed off by
the countless passing of unknown creatures. Due to the trail's good condition,
you are able to pass by this area quickly.
name: A Trail along a River

desc:
The trail continues alongside the river, bending here from west to north. The
path is sometimes obstructed by large boulders that have been dislodged from the
mountain to the south -- whether by natural forces or by the near-legendary
stone giants may never be known. The river to the north is inaccessible from
here because of the treacherous rocks that line its course.
name: A Trail along a River

desc:
The path rises to the north, beginning a gentle climb up the mountainside.
Southwards it bends gently, into a forested vale. The ground is littered by
small rocks, haphazardly strewn about the path. The pine trees, common to the
south, are sparser, and they no longer block the wind. Tough, dark- green grass
grows out from under the stones.
name: Mossy Path

desc:
Though this is still perhaps one of the more pleasant and well-built trails you
have travelled, time and the harsh clime have take their toll. Small boulders of
every colour and make, granite to lime, litter the path, and you have to take
care in stepping among them.
name: Wide Trail

desc:
The trail dips down back into a sheltered nook, heading southwards. The
mountains are all around, and you enter a small valley, hidden from exposure on
all sides.
name: Trail

desc:
Before you unfolds a small, hidden valley. The walls are sheer and unclimbable,
and crumble unhealthily. It seems the wind and rain are forbidden from
disturbing the eerie placidness, and have left the rock old and unpolished. All
is quiet, but a tenebrous tension borne of unnatural stillness pervades the
whole place.
name: Valley Entrance

desc:
The mountainside angles here sharply, becoming more and more severe until a
sheer western cliff is reached. The rock is flaky and weak, and pieces detach
from the main mass to fall to a painful breaking far below. But as you ascend,
reaching the impassable part, the stone undergoes a change in character. It
becomes smoother and darker, and seems more healthy and solid.
name: Rock Slide

desc:
The ground underfoot is crumbling; the footing unsteady. Unclimbable valley
walls rise ominously all around, cradling the canyon in a firm grasp that is
almost suffocating. The rocky floor angles to the south, draining to a
water-worn sinkhole. A brisk descent leads to another small ravine.
name: Empty Valley

desc:
The ravine is quiet and dark, the dizzyingly high walls throwing an eternal
shadow over its worn stone features. The irregularity of the walls make a climb
upwards possible, where the sky can be seen, and the ravine continues to the
east. The floor is water-washed and covered with smooth pebbles and rocks, and a
white line shows that this was once a stream bed.
name: Narrow Ravine

desc:
As you reach deeper and deeper into this dark valley, the tranquillity and
silence seem to become more and more threatening. Though it looks deserted,
there is no way to know if anything has passed this way recently. And the quiet
is the quiet of watching eyes and motionless talons. You begin to feel like
something hunted.
name: Narrow Ravine

desc:
By bracing your back and flexing your legs, you inch your way down a long,
narrow chimney. The walls are fairly smooth, and there are few footholds, but as
long as you are careful, there is no real danger. However as you descend it
becomes darker and darker, very cool. The air down here seems to be of a
different order--stagnant and ancient.
name: Natural Chimney

desc:
This is a natural corridor. A straight, long, stone wall rises on the west, that
extends upwards a hundred feet--there to meet the shale-grey sky. A fold in the
northern edge reveals a small stone alcove, somewhat hidden from full view. The
floor slopes gently to the east, where it meets up with a flat plateau, the
first in a series of terraces.
name: Along a Stone Wall

desc:
The floor here is sunk about a yard and a half lower than the floor outside. The
room is square and small, obviously chiselled by skilled hands into such a
shape. However, the edges are soft and weathered now, and the walls stained
darkly by something. Some rusted clutter lies about, and a few rocks, but that
is all that is left here.
name: Square Niche

desc:
The sky overhead is dark, and barely visible. For the walls on the north and
south are high and seem to lean inwards, so that only a narrow sliver of sky can
be seen. The terrace surface is flat and wide, and composed of a hard, rough
bedrock. You can hop down to another terrace to the east or with a little climb,
traverse the slope to the west.
name: Flat Terrace

desc:
The terrace is more like a ledge. The sides have begun to crumble, and the
footing is a bit unsafe. A big crack bisects the ledge, leaving a hole about a
yard wide that goes a long long way down.
name: Terrace

desc:
You have reached what must be the deepest part of the valley, an area so quiet
and dark, that you almost disbelieve that you are outside. The floor is solid
bedrock, and is inexplicably tainted black. High, high above, you can see the
tops of the valley walls. They are mountain tops, blackened, and piercing the
walls with sharp tips. It is hard to relate the open sky with this suffocating
darkness down here. The valley continues to the east.
name: Valley Floor

desc:
The black tinge of the walls seems to become darker and darker, covering more
and more of the stone, and you know that to credit it to the growing darkness
would be a lie. But to lie to oneself doesn't seem like such a bad idea right
now. A shiver runs down your spine as you suddenly realise that whatever
inhabits this fell valley has perceived you!
name: Valley Floor

desc:
The valley is narrow but very tall. Great, blackened walls rise sharply on every
side, and you almost have to bend backward to see the top, where the tips of
ancient mountains are, surrounded by misty white clouds. The shade here is very
deep, and you cannot see very much sky at all.
name: Valley Floor

desc:
A little canyon branches from the main valley floor here. Like the rest of this
area, it is shady and cool, but the feeling of being watched abruptly disappears
as soon as you turn the bend.
name: Side Canyon

desc:
The valley is narrow but very tall. Great, blackened walls rise sharply on every
side, and you have to almost bend over backward to see the top, where the tips
of ancient mountains are, surrounded by misty white clouds. The shade here is
very deep, and you cannot see very much sky at all.
name: Valley Floor

desc:
The walls arch slightly overhead, tunnel-like, making this room even darker than
the eternal dusk all around. To the north or south are large, open valleys.
name: Narrow Passage

desc:
You duck your head to enter this small chamber. The walls are roughly hewn and
shadows, conjured by your light's inadequate flame, dance wildly in the corners.
Opposite from the entrance, a half dozen large oval holes stare blankly from the
irregular rock wall.
name: Burial Cave

desc:
The air is completely still--almost stagnant--but thirty yards above the
mountain bushes dance contortedly in heavy wind, to the accompaniment of a great
howling. The ledge is itself high above the valley surface. and a quick glance
down makes you clutch the security of the rock dizzily. A rough, natural path
leads west to a small, dark hole in the stone.
name: High Ledge

desc:
You squeeze through the narrow hole and emerge into a much larger cave. It is
very dark in here, but what little light there is reveals a long, but thin
chamber, natural and irregular. The northern wall is riddled with a series of
dark holes, whose contents cannot be seen.
name: Burial Cave

desc:
Here the ceiling opens up, and a rocky route starts directly overhead. To the
east, the ground slopes downward quite rapidly, into a deeper darkness that
hides whatever lurks below.
name: Narrow Passage

desc:
A patter of gravel tumbles down the slope as you stumble and almost take the
quick way to the bottom. The footing here is slippery and dangerous, caused by
the loose layer of small rocks. The passage is made easier by keeping in touch
with the smooth northern wall. Down below, at the beginning of the incline, you
can see the entrance to a large cave.
name: Sunken Slope

desc:
The ground here crunches loudly as your boots sink into the gravelly ground with
each step. Each footfall makes you more and more paranoid, and facing the empty,
blank stare of cave entrance doesn't ease your nerves by any means. Unclimbable,
smooth walls rise about on all sides, and a slippery climb up is your only
alternative to the cave.
name: Sunken Slope

desc:
The lower mountainside here has split open, as if great stony hands had grasped
both sides and wrenched the rock apart. The ripped wall shows many layers of
multicoloured stone, not at all like the blackened scab covering everywhere
else. You waver momentarily before the gaping fissure, and your movement causes
some gravel to scrape together. A great force pulls you forward, like the urge
to jump from the edge of a height, or the call of the flame to the doomed moth.
name: Crevice

desc:
This endless tunnel burrows deep into the earth, seeming to penetrate the very
heart of the ancient mountain. It seems like you are passing into a new world;
one that is cold, lightless, and, but for the scuffle of your own feet, totally
quiet. There seems to be an eerie hum permeating the tunnel, but you know it is
merely the marks of your mind struggling to cope with the subterranean and alien
environment.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel looks as if it continues on what for seems like hours, before it ends
and pulls up before a huge, grim iron portal. The door is ten feet tall and half
as much in width, but seems to be very thick and sturdy. It swings to the left
on ponderous, roughly-hewn iron hinges, and remarkably is almost silent. In the
exact middle of the door is a massive keyhole, at least a foot in height.
name: Before the Iron Door

desc:
You enter a huge room with high, vaulted ceilings fifty feet overhead. The
flagstoned floor sprawls out before you, and your torch paints strange pictures
on the plain walls. There is an unfinished quality to the chamber, like the
seven empty niches cut out of the wall, and the unused tapestry knobs. High
above you can see the beginnings of an elaborate scene painted with faded dyes
on the ceiling, but the true colours and meaning of the diorama have passed from
this world.
name: Entrance Hall

desc:
You pass through a high, smooth stone arch into a tunnel-like room. The walls
are sculpted strangely, and you are both disgusted and strangely fascinated by
the grotesque patterns. Suddenly you realise in a horrible moment that what you
are looking at is not carved rock, but the actual petrified bones and skulls of
people, grafted somehow with the very walls.
name: Catacombs

desc:
Cautiously inching your way into this room, you see four passages ahead of you.
One or two of them are sealed or blocked off; intentionally or not, you can't be
sure. You turn around and then back again, and then you realise that you're not
sure from which way you came. The contorted bones hanging on the walls grin at
you.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
Cautiously inching your way into this room, you see four passages ahead of you.
One or two of them are sealed or blocked off; intentionally or not, you can't be
sure. You turn around and then back again, and then you realise that you're not
sure from which way you came. The contorted bones hanging on the walls grin at
you.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
Cautiously inching your way into this room, you see four passages ahead of you.
One or two of them are sealed or blocked off; intentionally or not, you can't be
sure. You turn around and then back again, and then you realise that you're not
sure from which way you came. The contorted bones hanging on the walls grin at
you.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The walls here seem to undergo a change from the ones before. These seem to be
newer, more recent additions to the ancient resting place. And more oddly, the
bones grafted into the stone are not wholly petrified, even though the process
has begun. Ragged strips of fabric, desiccated and rotted, hang on some of the
skeletons, and here and there you see the dull grey of iron or the glint of
steel.
name: Catacombs

desc:
You enter a plain, square room with walls of clean smooth stone and a slightly
rounded ceiling. Hanging on the walls are an assortment of martial tools,
including crossed black swords and long steel spears. The weapons, as well as
the two tapestries, are in surprisingly good condition, due perhaps to the
preserving effect of the lack of water and outside contamination.
name: Ancient Chamber

desc:
A set of wide, shallow stairs leads up to an open room. The room is raised up on
a dais and is bathed in shadows. Still, a sort of unholy light emanates strongly
from there, and you sense a great evil power.
name: Wide Stairs

desc:
When you enter this fell chamber you instantly know you have crossed into a
place of great evil and danger. The walls here are rusted black, a scabrous
lichen-substance covering the stone and fixtures. The flagstones are scratched
and cut, as by the assault of fearsome sharp talons. Resting against the far
wall is an immense throne of iron, sturdy as the mountain itself, and just as
immovable. You are overcome by a wave of fear and feel that you are in a
horribly precarious, dangerous place.
name: Raised Dais

desc:
As you break the seal and open the door, a blast of dusty, stale air puffs into
your face, momentarily blinding you. When you open your eyes, you see a small
five foot square room, with several stone chests lying against the western side
of the room.
name: Hidden Room

desc:
As the trail ends, a yawning indentation opens up to the south, where the rock
has been hewn away in large, cracked pieces. Among the layer of smooth
water-polished rubble is a collection of huge cube-shaped blocks of granite. The
mountainside has been systematically chipped away, and the exposed insides are
of the same, good rock as the blocks.
name: Splintered Mountainside

desc:
At the crest of this particular slope, a wide, sturdy ledge of solid rock
protrudes from the mountainside, and allows east-west travel. Though the wind is
loud and powerful, if you stay close to the mountain there is little danger. But
past the ledge it falls off steeply, into a rocky plain hundreds of yards below.
name: Trail Up the Mountain

desc:
A rudely carved channel in the rock here, perhaps an ancient streambed, allows
passage up the side of the mountain, which slopes steeply. Except for the long
rut, all else is smooth and impassable. Small, smooth stones tumble down
periodically.
name: Mountainside

desc:
You slip into a small niche--a jagged crack in the rock. Some geological forces
have pulled and stretched the sheer cliff face until it split open, and now a
dark cave looks out quietly.
name: Rock Niche

desc:
Soft, chalky hills undulate all about. It is hard on the eye, and you find
yourself falling asleep even as you walk. It would be so nice to just lay down
for a while and rest... to close heavy eyelids for just a moment. It seems like
there is no end to this place.
name: Hills

desc:
Dead, grey hills are heaped around the foot of the mountains like some dusty
piles of chalk. Indeed, the mounds here are of a weird, uncomfortable character.
The surface is loose and breaks easily, allowing little foothold for traveller
or vegetation. No trees choose to remain here, no grass wreathes the bloodless
hills.
name: Hills

desc:
A large, steep promontory juts out into the hills, separating the hills on the
west from the ones of the east. It is a subtle change. If possible, the eastern
hills are less threatening, but also, less alive. The grey of the east is not
the grey of dead grass; it is the grey of earth that has never had grass, of
flat sheets of stone masquerading as hillsides. A quick clamber up the side of
the promontory reveals this area in all its dark and dreary splendour. From way
up here the sprawling expanse before you takes on a sort of beauty, like a cold
goddess who disdains to smile.
name: Promontory

desc:
The land about rises higher and higher, affording a good view of a land perhaps
not worthy to be seen. Westerly, a dark and thick forest of pines grows among
the hills. Far to the east and south, past the grey hills, are the Misty
Mountains. And again, far to the north are more of the same.
name: Hills

desc:
The darkness hides the edges of the cave, but you can see that though it is not
too large, it is irregular, and dangerous. The ground, however, is fairly even,
and matted down with dirt and dead grass.
name: Cave

desc:
Cresting the rise of yet another round, grey, hill, a singular thought strikes
you. Out here in this wilderness, many miles from civilization or the company of
others--the loneliness, and savage, untamed nature of it all is overpowering.
There are no footprints in the chalky earth, no burnt-out campfires or discarded
horseshoes. Perhaps you are the first to ever venture here. Certainly there is
no reason in sight to draw visitors, or even explorers. There is not a soul to
be seen for miles and miles in this depressing wasteland.
name: Hills

desc:
A wide, flat promontory intrudes shockingly into the heart of the grey hills,
glowering ominously at the rest of the landscape. The ledge is shallow and not
too high, but flat and composed of a hard, dark rock. It isn't too hard to
scramble up the side and get to the other side... more hills.
name: Wide Ridge

desc:
You have descended into a little flat dell between a very high hill, and a
jutting, low escarpment of dark rock. Here is shaded and protected from the
periodic winds, and the grass grows thickly. Some hardy bushes perch on the
leeward side, and you even see some tough little wildflowers waving in the wind,
their plain yellow and white colours a delight in this desolate land.
name: A Flat Dell

desc:
The ravine continues along in an west-east direction, a great rut furrowed into
the hard brown granite. The sides are too steep to see over into the surrounding
lands, but to the south, a grim landscape of bleached white hills waits
disinterestedly. Scratchy, dense thorn bushes throw thin, spiny claws in your
way.
name: Ravine

desc:
The bush-filled canyon spills out into a rising area of rough, rocky ground, and
you can see the solid, immense wall of the mountains rising before you. It is
hard to tell exactly what dwells amongst the various dells and depressions here,
covered as they are by concealing foliage.
name: Ravine

desc:
Your path draws up in a rocky little valley with no other exits. The sides are
too high to climb, and there are no other paths leading out. The floor of the
gulch is dry and strewn with all manner of polished little pebbles and stones.
It seems that once there was a small pond here, but now all is dry and quiet.
However, a second glance reveals a small shelter behind some bushes. A campfire
has been used here not too long ago, and some tools are scattered about
haphazardly.
name: Blind Gulch

desc:
From here unfolds an impressive panorama of a dark wilderness, totally barren of
man or his devices. There is no trace of human habitation, not a building or a
road of any sort. All you can see are dark forests, white hills, grey marches,
and mountains of solid granite. And a deep ancient cold touches all of them with
icy fingers.
name: Cliff

desc:
A rocky ledge protrudes past the slope of the mountainside here, hiding the
misty tops from view but offering somewhat of a shelter from the weather. A
natural chimney, the sides rough and rocky, give easy access up.
name: Overhang

desc:
This is a long, smooth slope, covered with half-frozen grass. It is not hard to
go east or west, and to the south a large ravine splits the earth. An old animal
trail leads to the north, but then meanders off back west.
name: Mountains

desc:
A long, wide ravine opens to the south and west. The sides are tall and slant at
a 45 degree angle to meet the grim, grey sky. The ravine floor is covered by a
thick growth of tough bushes that make it hard to traverse. There is no sign of
any human life, except for a rough, overgrown trail. You get the feeling that it
was made by animals, perhaps wolves or some species of northern elk. However,
far above, near the rim of the ravine, there is a long, white scratch of chalk.
name: Ravine

desc:
Unlike the other mounds this great hill is tall and flat, with sharp, steeply
rising sides. It is bald, and the whitened platform of the top catches the glare
the of the unkind distant sun. A precipitous cliff to the west descends into the
depths of a dark pine forest, from whence emits a cacophony of imagined forest
dangers. In all other directions are monotonous hills.
name: A High, Flattened Hill

desc:
The forest here is dark, but patches of weak light filter down through the
canopy. The less dense nature of the woods must be the cause of the relative
brightness. To the west the trees thicken malignantly, like the muscular
contraction of a great serpent's coils. Off to the east can be seen large, grey
hills, that you aren't sure are any more inviting.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The bitter air that sluggishly wafts through the glades and openings of the
forest is crisply frigid, and surprisingly fresh. But it is dark here, and if it
weren't for the tiny light of your little lantern, you wouldn't have a chance of
staying on the path. As it is, rocks and roots reach for your legs from the
side, and ruts in the path open up to trip and hinder your passage.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The river rushes through the narrow gorge with a thundering tumult. The walls
press closer together, causing the current to rush even faster. Threatening
boulders that have managed somehow to resist the current loom as the current
rushes past, promising certain death if struck.
name: On a River

desc:
Huge stone walls border the river both north and south, imprisoning it though
the river rages against its captors. The current is treacherously swift,
threatening to dash any boats against the sharp rocks.
name: On a River

desc:
The river is shallow here, but still swift enough to be a danger to the care-
less traveller. To the west, the river enters a gorge -- steep rocky cliffs rise
on both side -- and to the east it continues toward its source. The trail that
follows the southern bank is accessible here -- sharp rocks prevent access
farther upstream and downstream.
name: On a River

desc:
The river grows smaller and shallower to the east as it approaches its head-
waters. Several small streams join it here, and the volume of water is sig-
nificantly greater to the west. Sharp rocks and treacherous eddies in the
current prevent one from accessing the trail that still follows the river to the
south.
name: On a River

desc:
The river makes a hairpin turn from south to west here, following a natural gap
between two mountains. The source of this water cannot lie too much far- ther
upstream -- it hardly merits the designation river at this point. To the south,
a gigantic natural bridge made of a single block of granite dis- lodged from the
mountainside spans the river.
name: On a River

desc:
The trail winds along the rim of a shallow gorge, through which the river to the
north makes its way. Any slack in alertness may prove dangerous, for the trail
is very narrow and in some places slick with spray rising from small cataracts
in the river.
name: A Trail along a River

desc:
The trail forks here, with a small and faint spur leading down to the east into
a canyon. The main trail turns north, following the path of the turbulent river.
The only vegetation in this sombre landscape are bushes that possess wicked
thorns, which grab and tear at all exposed clothing.
name: A Trail along a River

desc:
The river to the north is passing through a shallow gorge, while the trail winds
along on the rim. The trail turns from south to west here, following the river
as best it can. To the east, a large ridge rises to the south of the trail,
sealing it between the mountainside and the river.
name: A Trail along a River

desc:
The trail is very precarious indeed, poised along a gorge to the north and a
looming mountainside to the south. Sometimes boulders from frequent rock- slides
almost completely block the path, forcing those who wish to pass into
dangerously scrambling over the debris. To the east, the gorge seems to be
nearing its head, and the trail begins to head down toward the edge of the
river.
name: A Trail along a River

desc:
The head of the gorge lies just to the east, and the sheer rock cliff that lies
to the south of the trail seems to end there as well. The trail is often
littered with debris from frequent rockslides, but at least it is much wider
here than farther to the west.
name: A Trail along a River

desc:
The trail again runs right along the river here, and due to the caprice of
nature, the sharp rocks that usually border the riverbed are not as frequent
here, allowing access to the cold water. To the south is a smaller trail that
leads down into a canyon that lies to the south of the ridge that begins just
west of here. The trail itself continues east and west, leaving the river
briefly in the latter direction.
name: A Trail along a River

desc:
Just to the east, the river bends slightly, and the trail takes advantage of a
magnificent natural granite bridge to cross the shallow gorge. To the west, the
trail continues to follow every twist of the river, eventually leading out of
the Misty Mountains and into the Ettenmoors.
name: A Trail along a River

desc:
This massive rock slab has fortuitously formed a bridge across the river's
gorge, a fact taken advantage of by the trail. Below runs the river, still
daunting despite being so near to its source. A small ledge situated along the
western cliff below the bridge might allow one access to the river, but the
currents there are bound to be dangerous.
name: A Sloping Bridge

desc:
This trail cuts between two large peaks, leaving a river to the north and
heading south deeper into the mountains. The air is permanently chilled here,
and the rarefied atmosphere makes breathing laborious. To the south the road
seems to head down slightly, before twisting out of sight.
name: A Dangerous Road

desc:
Many years ago, a lively mountain stream flowed through here. Now all that
remains is a narrow trail strewn with rocks. To the west, a narrow cleft cuts
into the cliff face -- the stream obviously once issued from that direction. The
sides of this narrow valley are far to steep to be scaled, so the only ways to
proceed are to enter the cleft or turn back.
name: Dry Streambed

desc:
The mountain path is nothing more than a mountain ledge here. The path continues
to the south and to the west, being much wider there. It is very hard to move
safely and impossible to ride a mount of any kind. Some strange tracks can be
found going southwards. The path continues ahead leading to what seems like the
abode of some unknown creatures.
name: Narrow Ledge

desc:
You can see a grand view from this summit; your eyes wander far to the west,
over the foothills and the forests below; perhaps that is the Mitheithel on the
horizon. There is no obvious path downwards, but it is possible to negotiate a
ridge that leads toward the south.
name: On Top of a Mountain

desc:
This wide shelf houses an eyrie. The nest itself is foul, covered with filth and
rotting meat, indicating that these eagles are not kin to the noble line of
Thorondor. Perhaps when Morgoth held sway over Middle-earth while the Valar
dwelt in Valinor, he bred such corrupt birds in mockery of the eagles of Manwe
that dwell upon high Taniquetil.
name: An Eagle's Eyrie

desc:
This path does not look too safe. Having the heart of a lion is not all that it
takes to survive these parts. Especially since your mounts do not seem very
happy with the rocky and treacherous route. Up ahead the path becomes even more
dangerous and you pause and wonder if it is worth the risk.
name: A Mountain Path

desc:
This narrow space cuts between two sheer rock faces that are separated by a mere
ten metres. No vegetation grows here -- the ground is very rocky and strewn with
boulders. To the east, the cliffs give way to an open space, while the cleft
continues to the north, getting narrower with each step in that direction.
name: Narrow Cleft

desc:
The narrow cleft in the mountainside at last comes to an end. In every dir-
ection save south rise steep rock faces -- seemingly impossible to scale. The
ground here is shaded except during the middle of the day, and there are several
large boulders lying strewn about, in fact, some of them appear to have been
deliberately piled in front of a shallow recess in the rock.
name: Back of Cleft

desc:
You seem to be at a bend in the path. You can't see more than a few dozen feet
ahead of you. So you feel that there might be lots of hidden eyes staring at you
from hidden places. The sooner you cross the mountains, the better. You keep
whispering to yourself "Keep alert! Don't sleep!", over and over. The path to
the north seems to lead to some shelter far away.
name: A Mountain Path

desc:
On and on you go further into the mountains. You feel that these mountains are
enchanted. You hope that the enchantment is good magic and not the filthy magic
of the Enemy. This path seems to be not that well used and hence you feel it
will be safe from the Lidless Eye's minions.
name: A Mountain Path

desc:
You are on a path that is fairly wide here. East of here the path becomes very
narrow and dangerous. Care should be taken while crossing it. To the west you
can leave the path and get onto the mountain, but north seems to be the most
interesting. From here, the mountain tops seem to be covered with fog, and the
whole place looks very gloomy and sad.
name: A Mountain Path

desc:
Directly to the east of here is a gravel-covered slope leading steeply down to
the floor of a shallow canyon. The small trail comes to an end here, so the only
alternative to returning to the south is to attempt the slope. Getting down
should not be a problem, but it may be impossible to return up here.
name: Ledge above Canyon

desc:
To the north a large and rocky ridge blocks the way, but the canyon wall to the
south looks relatively easy to scale. To the west, the mouth of the canyon meets
with the river, and presumably the trail as well. To the east the canyon widens
and becomes shallower.
name: Narrow Canyon

desc:
The sheer rock face of a granite ridge forms the inpregnable northern wall of
this canyon. To the south, the ground slopes gently upward for a while until the
steep southern wall is reached. The ridge to the north appears to end a bit to
the west, and the canyon also gets much shallower in that direction.
name: Narrow Canyon

desc:
Here you can enter the trail running east-west if you go north. To the west the
canyon becomes very narrow. You feel as if there is danger to the south of you.
You feel you really ought to investigate the region to the south though it would
mean climbing a few rocks and boulders and even small hills.
name: Wide Canyon

desc:
Having come south of the trail and the adjoining canyon, you see that the ground
rises here in a gentle slope, and abruptly becomes a very high rocky wall
preventing you from going south. You can go east though where the landscape is
similar to around here.
name: A Gentle Slope

desc:
This gentle slope ends abruptly to the south in a granite cliff. To the east it
may be possible to scramble up the cliff as it is not as steep in that
direction. To the north is the floor of a canyon carved many years ago by a
river that has long since abandoned this channel. To the west the terrain looks
very similar, though only for a short distance as another steep slope can be
seen.
name: A Gentle Slope

desc:
You are at the northern edge of a wide canyon. You see that the canyon becomes
narrower if you go north and west. To your south there are a few hills and
boulders. In the distance you see a daunting mountain. Suddenly you feel that
maybe it will not be as good an idea as you thought at first.
name: Wide Canyon

desc:
This trail takes advantage of a narrow flat area between two towering peaks to
the east and west. To the north, the trail rises higher while it leads to lower
elevations to the south. The hard and rocky soil bears a few foot- prints --
someone has been here not long ago.
name: A Dangerous Road

desc:
Here again the steep slope continues. There seem to be a lot of nooks and
perches in these slopes where an ambush party could be waiting to pounce on you
when you least expect it. You also seem to hear the sounds of eagles nearby,
though you don't see their homes. There does not seem to be a lot of places to
go from here except west.
name: A Steep Slope

desc:
This narrow ledge gives a brief respite to those attempting to climb the steep
scree-covered slope. Below, the slope is too steep to descend, as the slightest
misstep would send an unfortunate adventurer tumbling down to the canyon floor
below. It is possible to manoeuvre east and west along the ledge, and one can
probably climb up as well.
name: A Steep Slope

desc:
The steep mountain side continues here from the east. There is not much of a
hope of any way out of here but to continue the climb. The climb seems to get
more and more dangerous as you take each step. You regret your decision to climb
these slopes. You hope some of the great eagles of this region will take pity on
you and take you to safety but they are nowhere to be seen now.
name: A Steep Slope

desc:
Here the sounds of the great eagles sounds nearer. Maybe there is an eyrie
nearby. You wonder if the evil of Sauron has touched their souls yet. The only
hope for your mission to succeed is that they are still enemies of the one Enemy
and will aid you. Without their help, the path through these mountains might be
too difficult for you to overcome. If you do not find them, you will have to
continue your trudge upwards and hope your shoes do not fail you.
name: A Steep Slope

desc:
You find that at such heights, the air is very rare and your breathing has grown
deeper. Stories of people dying of lack of air come to mind. You shudder. Hey
what was that? An eagle. ... No, it is just an illusion of your frightened mind.
Steep slopes all around, and lots of hidden nooks. Enough to drive your crazed
mind out through the doors into the land of paranoia. Maybe you can rest nearby
for a while.
name: A Steep Slope

desc:
Aaaaaaahhhh. At last a place to rest your tired bones away from the eyes of
danger. This seems like the ideal place to rest and recuperate from the arduous
climb... or is it?! Your paranoia catches hold of you for a second but you
decide to take a chance. Looking around you see that you can watch the whole of
the road beneath you. This seems like the ideal spot to waylay travellers and
loot them of their money and such.
name: Hidden Perch

desc:
This narrow ledge is slick with spray spewn by the river as it rushes over the
rocky streambed. Directly above is a large block of granite that has been
sloughed off by the mountain -- it forms a natural bridge crossing the shallow
gorge through which the river runs. The trail that has been following the river
seems to cross here as well, so it has obviously been here for a long time.
name: Ledge

desc:
The trail bends to the east and south here. There is a dark valley to the north,
and far below you, which you might be able to reach safely. There is a large
plateau to your west, and off in the north you can see the higher peaks of the
Misty Mountains. When you look close, you can clearly see that there is some
sort of bird flying about the peaks.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
This fairly steep slope leads straight up into the mountains. Looking toward the
sky, one can see some large birds circling around the peaks. The ground is
extremely rough here, branch and stone. One wrong step could send an unwary
traveller tumbling down the hill, for the mountains are unkind and fraught with
danger.
name: Slope up the Mountain

desc:
You are on a very steep slope leading up to the mountains. You can see the
beginnings of a forest to your south, and think you can walk down the slope to
reach it. You can also head north, heading further up the mountain and into the
unknown.
name: Slope up the Mountain

desc:
You are at the edge of the forest. The trees are just beginning to thicken now,
and the ground is getting softer as you continue into the forest. As you look
into the heart of the woods, it appears that the trees are moving around, as if
they are trying to corral some new prey.
name: Edge of the Misty Forest

desc:
You have reached the eastern edge of the valley, and looking through the mist,
you can see that the ground slopes up to the east, leading right into the
mountains. As you look into the valley you can see yellow eyes peering at you
from the mist, and wonder which direction is the lesser of all evils.
name: Misty Valley

desc:
You are right in the middle of the valley, and it is especially dark and damp
here. The grass is knee high, and you think you feel things crawling along your
leg. When you look up, instead of seeing sky you can only see the branches of
large trees looming overhead.
name: Misty Valley

desc:
This valley is extremely dark for the thick mist blocks out most of the sky.
This valley is quite green though - perhaps a result of all the moisture
accumulated here. One cannot be certain, but it almost sounds like growling off
in the distance.
name: Misty Valley

desc:
The mist in this valley makes everything very dark, and your light reflects off
the fog, making small rainbows wherever you look. Despite this fascinating
effect, everything about this valley makes you nervous, especially when you hear
a loud growling in the distance.
name: Misty Valley

desc:
You are at the edge of the forest. The trees are just beginning to thicken now,
and the ground is getting softer as you continue into the forest. As you look
into the heart of the woods, it appears that the trees are moving around, as if
they are trying to corral some new prey.
name: Edge of the Misty Forest

desc:
You are in the middle of a forest, and can't see a damn thing! The mist is so
thick here that you can almost see your reflection in it. When you look at the
trees, they seem to be shifting about, and you can't tell which direction you
are going, or even came from.
name: Misty Forest

desc:
You are in the middle of a forest, and can't see a damn thing! The mist is so
thick here that you can almost see your reflection in it. When you look at the
trees, they seem to be shifting about, and you can't tell which direction you
are going, or even came from.
name: Misty Forest

desc:
After such a long climb, you are almost at the top of the mountains. However,
judging from the size of the birds that you see there, it might be good idea
just to turn around and head back to the safety of the plateau.
name: Near the Top

desc:
You are on a very steep slope, and you have to be very careful of your footing
here, because one wrong step and you could be bouncing into the valley far below
you. You can head up to the top of the mountain, but after noticing the large
birds up there, it might be a better idea to just head back down into the
valley.
name: Slope up the Mountain

desc:
This valley is directly in the shadow of the mountains, and between that and the
trees, it is nearly impossible to see beyond your light. There is very little
vegetation here because of the lack of light, so the only animals around must be
carnivorous....
name: Dark Valley

desc:
You are in a very dark valley, but not sure exactly where, because it is so dark
that it is easy to get turned around. The trees here loom overhead, blocking out
all shreds of light. There are many track marks on the ground, many which you
recognise as canine, yet larger than any dog you have seen before.
name: Dark Valley

desc:
This valley is directly in the shadow of the mountains, and between that and the
trees, it is nearly impossible to see beyond your light. There is hardly any
vegetation here, so the noises you hear behind you must be coming from
carnivores.
name: Dark Valley

desc:
The ground gets very steep here, and you have to be careful to not slip and
slide down the slope. There is a dark valley to your south, which is shadowed by
the mountains and some very large trees.
name: Slope up the Mountain

desc:
You are on a somewhat steep slope that leads directly up to the mountains.
Looking up, you can see some large birds flying around the peaks. You should
probably be careful of where you walk, because the ground is very rough here,
and one wrong step could send you sliding down the hill.
name: Slope up the Mountain

desc:
You are in a very dark valley, but not sure exactly where, because it is so dark
that it is easy to get turned around. The trees here loom overhead, blocking out
all shreds of light. There are many track marks on the ground, many which you
recognise as canine, yet larger than any dog you have seen before.
name: Dark Valley

desc:
You are on a flat plateau that overlooks the trail to the east, and a dark
valley to the north. The mountains loom to your west and north, making you want
to stick to the safety of the plateau. There aren't very many animals around
here, even though there is an abundance of plant life.
name: Plateau

desc:
You are in the middle of a flat plateau. You can see the mountains far to the
north, and the Angmarian Trail further to your east. Even though there is
abundant vegetation here, there aren't very many animals. It seems strange to
you....
name: Plateau

desc:
You are still on this flat plateau. The walking here is very easy, even though
you do have to step over many fallen trees and other bushes. You notice that the
trees did not fall down naturally, but judging from all the claw marks, some
very large creature was the cause.
name: Plateau

desc:
You are still on this flat plateau, yet now there is a lone tree emerging from
the ground. The tree is leaveless, and the branches barely seem to be able to
hold their own weight. However, now you finally know the cause of the lack of
animals, and you should leave soon if you don't want to be just another carcass
on the ground.
name: A Lone Ragged Tree

desc:
You are on a flat, dry, and rocky plateau that heads further west. If you
choose, you can go following the sloping ground east, which leads straight into
the mountains.
name: Broad Plateau

desc:
You are in a broad plateau, which is quite flat in comparison to the mountains
to the east. The terrain is very rocky here, and it is quite possible that
anything might come out from behind the next boulder.
name: Broad Plateau

desc:
This plateau is very barren, and no matter which direction you look you can only
see more flat ground. There is little vegetation here, and each step you take
makes small dust clouds come up from under your boots.
name: Barren Plateau

desc:
The plateau simply ends here. If you want to continue west here, you might be in
for a long fall, because the drop is extremely steep and rocky. There is nowhere
to go except back the way you came.
name: End of the Plateau

desc:
You are on a path which heads further north, or east into a fog covered forest.
You are not sure what is in the forest, but every time you look at it the trees
seem to move a little, as if they were closing in on some new prey.
name: Path to the Valley

desc:
The ground here flattens out quite a bit, and the walk isn't that bad. There
isn't very much vegetation here, and what little you see reminds you of the
carnivorous plants. The plateau continues all directions, but you only see more
of the same, flat, dusty ground.
name: Broad Plateau

desc:
You have finally reached the end of the path. The only thing left until you
reach the top is the long, difficult climb. The air is very thin here, and the
stench permeating from above is very strong now. You still doubt whether or not
to continue, but the urge to explore is too strong to stop now.
name: Path around the Mountain

desc:
You are in what appears to be the former den of a very large bird. The birds
seem to be gone though, and their only remains are a large pile of old feathers
and bones on the ground. The view from this perch is outstanding...you can see
for many miles in every direction.
name: Eagles Watch

desc:
You are on a path that is slowly winding its way up the mountain. It is starting
to get hard to breathe now, because of the lack of oxygen, and the horrible
odour which is coming from just a little further down the path. Looking closely,
you notice some scratches on one of the larger rocks.
name: Path to the Mountain

desc:
You are on a path which leads you west up to the mountain, or east into a
fog-shrouded valley. You can't see into the valley, because a thick layer of
mist covers everything except the tips of the trees, which peer at you like
large, green eyes.
name: Path to the Valley

desc:
You are on a path which continues to wind up the mountain. Each step is painful
as you step on rocks, stones, and what appears to be the bones of humans and
various other animals. You wonder whether or not you really want to find out
what is at the top of the mountain.
name: Path around the Mountain

desc:
You are on a path which continues to wind its way up the mountain. The path
heads south here, and you are provided with an excellent view of what is below
you, including a very strange looking circle of trees, which is surrounded by an
eerie fog.
name: Path around the Mountain

desc:
You are at the edge of the broad plateau. You can see many hills to the west,
but would need to grow wings to go that way, because there is no way to walk
down without being seriously injured.
name: Edge of the Plateau

desc:
You have reached the edge of the plateau. You can't go any further west, but
there seems to be some tracks heading to the east...the only thing is, they
don't look humanoid.
name: Edge of the Plateau

desc:
The ground is quite rocky here, which is a good change from the rest of the
plateau. You can climb up to a ridge to the east, or continue on into the
plateau. As you look to the east, you can see the mountains, and catch a glimpse
of something flying around the higher spires.
name: Broad Plateau

desc:
Finally, you are seeing something other than dust and decay. The ground here is
beginning to get quite rocky, and there are many rocks and boulders scattered
about. As you look around one boulder, you realise what a good hiding place they
make, and wonder what is hiding behind the next boulder.
name: Broad Plateau

desc:
The ground here starts to slope up to the east, in anticipation of the
mountains. Everything around is very dusty, and you are wondering what happened
to all the vegetation around here.
name: Barren Plateau

desc:
The land is very flat and dry here. As you look at the mountains and see all of
the water on the mountains, you wonder how there could not be more vegetation
here. It looks like there used to be a small pond here, but it has long since
dried up.
name: Broad Plateau

desc:
You are on the edge of a broad plateau. There is no way to continue further west
because the land drops off suddenly. However, the drop does give you a good view
of the lands to the west, and you can make out several campfires in the
distance, and wonder what type of creature is camping in this desolate area.
name: Edge of the Plateau

desc:
You are on a narrow ridge that overlooks a wide plateau to the west. On your
east, you can see the mountains looming overhead. You wonder to yourself whether
or not you really want to explore them any further.
name: Narrow Ridge

desc:
You can explore the ridge further to the east or south, or walk down to the
plateau below. The plateau looks extremely dry, and you can't see a hint of
green anywhere.
name: Narrow Ridge

desc:
You are on a narrow ridge that overlooks a wide plateau to the west. On your
east, you can see the mountains looming overhead. You wonder to yourself whether
or not you really want to explore them any further.
name: Narrow Ridge

desc:
You stand on a narrow ridge overlooking a wide plateau to the west. The eastern
horizon is dominated by the tall spires of the neighbouring mountains. Their
daunting heights makes you rethink whether you should continue and brave the
treacherous terrain.
name: Narrow Ridge

desc:
You are on a small, extremely hazardous path in the Misty Mountains. The
mountains rise up on all sides of you, and no matter which way you travel you
are in for a treacherous climb, because it seems that the rocks could slip at
any moment, plunging you down the mountain buried under tons of rock.
name: Path through the Misty Mountains

desc:
You are on a small, extremely hazardous path in the Misty Mountains. The
mountains rise up on all sides of you, and no matter which way you travel you
are in for a treacherous climb, because it seems that the rocks could slip at
any moment, plunging you down the mountain buried under tons of rock.
name: Path through the Misty Mountains

desc:
You are on a small, extremely hazardous path in the Misty Mountains. The
mountains rise up on all sides of you, and no matter which way you travel you
are in for a treacherous climb, because it seems that the rocks could slip at
any moment, plunging you down the mountain buried under tons of rock.
name: Path through the Misty Mountains

desc:
You are on a small, extremely hazardous path in the Misty Mountains. The
mountains rise up on all sides of you, and no matter which way you travel you
are in for a treacherous climb, because it seems that the rocks could slip at
any moment, plunging you down the mountain buried under tons of rock.
name: Path through the Misty Mountains

desc:
You are inside a huge crack in the ridge, which is plenty wide enough for
someone twice your size. The walls here are very unstable, and while you should
be able to climb down quite easily, coming back up could be problem.
name: Split in the Ridge

desc:
You are on a very large and distinctive outcropping of rock. You pause a second
to think of where to go from here. Your reverie is broken by the sudden
realization that the rockface is like an elven face frozen in a horrific
expression of fear and anguish.
name: Rock Outcropping

desc:
You are on an outcropping of rock which overlooks the river valley far below.
There is absolutely no way that you are going to be able to get down from here.
You are just going to have to turn back the way you came.
name: Rocky Overhang

desc:
This ledge overlooks the valley and the ridge below you. The ground here is very
cracked and broken, almost as if an earthquake had hit here. However, the ground
is very stable, even if it is painful to walk on.
name: Broken Ledge

desc:
You are at the end of the ridge here. You think you can climb up to a ledge
here, because the cliff face is very broken and full of all kinds of handholds.
You can see down into the valley from here too, and seeing the green grass
reminds you of much better days.
name: Broad Ridge

desc:
The valley ends here, and there is a large overhang to your east. The ledge is
much to high to reach from here though. If you want to get up there, you will
have to find a different way to go about it.
name: River Valley

desc:
The valley starts to slope up here, and the grass isn't quite as green as it is
in other areas. You look at the mountains, and get a strong feeling that the
reason for the change is the evil presence emanating from the north.....
name: River Valley

desc:
You are on a broad ridge which overlooks the river valley. To the north, you can
see a large circle of trees. You can't tell what is in the middle though...some
sort of mist is blocking your view.
name: Broad Ridge

desc:
You are on a broad, flat ridge overlooking the river valley. The ground here is
very easy to walk on, which surprises you since you are so close to the
mountains. As you look down and see the river, it reminds you of how long its
been since you have taken a nice refreshing dip in the water.
name: Broad Ridge

desc:
