This would be the most beautiful place you have ever been, if it weren't for the
ominous mountains to your north. The grass here is soft and green, and would be
a perfect place for a nap. There is a small pool of water here, probably excess
runoff from the mountains.
name: River Valley

desc:
You are in a lush river valley. For the first time in many miles, you feel at
peace. However, one look north at the mountains reminds you of the dangers which
surround you.
name: River Valley

desc:
Plain and simple, this place stinks! The walls are covered with furs of various
animals, including some wargs. There are bits and pieces of bone and flesh
scattered all over the floor, making footing slippery and treacherous.
name: Smelly Cave

desc:
You are right underneath the large ridge you were just walking on. You can climb
back up if you want, but not sure if it would be a great idea. As the wind howls
above, it draws with it a stench from the west, which reminds you of rotting
meat.
name: Underneath the Ridge

desc:
You are amidst a circle of trees, all which tower above you like hideous giants.
You attempt to see into the centre of the circle, but a strange mist blocks your
view, and the only thing you can make out is a slight hint of movement.
name: Circle of Trees

desc:
Reaching the centre of the massive circle of trees, you enter an area of thick
and cloying fog. The fog seems almost alive, its sinuous tendrils reaching
around to caress your clammy skin. An illogical fear builds in your soul, and
you wonder how long you can stay here before sheer terror forces you to leave.
name: Centre of the Circle

desc:
You are amidst a circle of trees, all which tower above you like hideous giants.
You attempt to see into the centre of the circle, but a strange mist blocks your
view, and the only thing you can make out is a slight hint of movement.
name: Circle of Trees

desc:
You are amidst a circle of trees, all which tower above you like hideous giants.
You attempt to see into the centre of the circle, but a strange mist blocks your
view, and the only thing you can make out is a slight hint of movement.
name: Circle of Trees

desc:
The trail leads north-south here. There is a broad plateau to the west, and in
the north you can make out the spires of the Misty Mountains. Looking at the
mountains gives you a great feeling of awe at their majesty, and a feeling of
dread of what lies within them.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
The trail leads north and east here. There is a broad plateau to the west, and
to the north you can make out the spires of the Misty Mountains. Looking at the
mountains gives you a great feeling of awe at their majesty, and a feeling of
dread at the thought of what lies within them.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
This tunnel is huge! You could easily fit four or five of you shoulder to
shoulder and have plenty of room. As you walk further down the path, you begin
to wonder exactly what is at the end of this journey.
name: Path under the Mountains

desc:
It was a bad idea to come down here. The smell here is twenty times worse than
it was up above. The coffin you are standing in the middle of has long rotted
away, and only its general shape remains. The bones of whomever was buried here
are still intact. You have an odd feeling that you and the corpse are not the
only ones here.
name: Decaying Grave

desc:
You are only able to get part way down the side of the mountain. You can go no
further because the terrain has become impossible to pass. The Misty Mountains
are east of here, but you can see no way to get to them. The only way out of
here is back up into the untamed mountains you just came from.
name: Part Way up the Misty Mountains

desc:
As you walk underneath the mountain, nagging worries prey upon your mind, and
you wonder if perhaps you should reconsider your exploration. A disgusting
crunching sound emanates from the end of the corridor, and your blood runs cold
as you realise it is the sound of bones being chewed.
name: Path under the Mountains

desc:
As you walk underneath the mountain, you begin to wonder whether or not this is
such a good idea. The east wall is covered with a sheet of bones, and your fear
is doubled when you hear a loud growling from the end of the tunnel, which
grates your ears and gives you shivers.
name: Path under the Mountains

desc:
You have stumbled onto some sort of secret cave lair. Bits of stone, bones, and
destroyed equipment litter the floor. A foul stench burns your nose and begin to
think this was not a good idea. The occupants seem to think the same thing, and
you prepare to defend yourself.
name: Stone Giants Refuge

desc:
Though it is now occupied by untidy giants, it seems they have not always been
here. Indeed this was once a complex piece of architecture, if what remains is
anything to go by. The high arched vaulting still remains intact though large
chunks have been smashed out. A dim glow emanates from these upper structures,
providing a low illumination. Subtle murals in dark blues and greens have been
filthied by the giants, while clots of blood stain the east wall where a
humanoid in robes has been driven into the wall. The giants have made themselves
quite comfortable, bringing crude chairs and bedding to this place, oblivious to
its wondrous nature. The smell is certainly strong.
name: Home of the Giants

desc:
This room has more of a natural feel to it than the vaulted chamber to the
south. Here the stone has been carved and manipulated, creating the impression
of being under the eaves of a rock forest. The summer sunlight glittering
through the trees, is here replaced by lichens which give off a green
phosphorescence on the ceiling. The rocky walls, carved like bark, have rivelets
of water snaking down their length, collecting in pools before leaching away
into cracks in the floor. The air in here is laden with moisture, cool and
still, yet spoiled by the invasive smell of giants.
name: The Dimly-lit Sanctum

desc:
The rushing torrent rages westward, threatening to sweep away all in its path.
The river is shallow and icy cold -- its source cannot be too much farther in
the mountains. The sides of the river are lined with slick and treacherous
rocks, precluded any hope of exiting it here.
name: On a River

desc:
The river pounds westward, leaving a shallow gorge to the east. The shallow
water is full of sharp rocks and dangerous rapids, so extreme caution is
required in order to successfully manoeuvre.
name: On a River
